qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated subscription notification panic crashes UDR
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a separate nil-pointer denial-of-service in internal/sbi/processor/callback.go's notification path, not the advisory's missing return in HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdGet that leaks subscription data on an invalid influenceId path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause: the missing return after c.String(http.StatusNotFound, "404 page not found") in HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdGet, which allows an invalid influenceId request to continue into the Traffic Influence Subscription read path.
evidence: The advisory describes an unauthenticated information disclosure in GET /nudr-dr/v2/application-data/influenceData/{influenceId}/{subscriptionId}, specifically when influenceId is not "subs-to-notify" but execution still reaches ApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdGetProcedure.

The only qualifying finding instead targets POST/PUT/PATCH subscription-data endpoints and reports a nil-pointer dereference in internal/sbi/processor/callback.go during notification handling, causing process termination.

The finding's code locations and exploit path do not mention api_datarepository.go, HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdGet, invalid influenceId path validation, or unauthorized reads of Traffic Influence Subscription objects.
The impacts differ materially: the advisory is confidentiality disclosure of subscription objects, while the finding is availability loss from a crash.
